mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.5% quarterly annualized confirms manufacturing sector resilience, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power during late-cycle expansions, with 5-day forward returns turning negative in >60% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or inflation spikes coincide with positive equity returns in >70% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units annually signal strong construction demand and wealth effects that lift equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of threshold-crossing months, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect reverses during labor market overheating, yielding negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates real rate normalization that stabilizes market expectations, triggering positive short-term S&P 500 momentum.
failure_condition: Signal fails during stagflationary episodes where 5-day returns remain negative despite widening real rate spreads.
